requirements.title: Async IO Root Structure
requirements.description: The async IO root shall embed a module root with an API vtable pointer.
requirements.rationale: An abstract async IO interface decouples application-level communication logic from platform-specific UART, socket, or DMA implementations, enabling portability and testability.

requirements.title: Async IO Read
requirements.description: The async IO API vtable shall define a Read operation that reads up to the requested number of bytes and updates the size with bytes actually read.
requirements.rationale: Read with actual-bytes-read feedback allows callers to handle partial reads, which is the norm for non-blocking and DMA-based IO on embedded targets.

requirements.title: Async IO TryRead
requirements.description: The async IO API vtable shall define a TryRead operation that reads with a timeout in microseconds.
requirements.rationale: TryRead with a timeout enables bounded-time read attempts, which is essential for real-time systems that cannot block indefinitely on IO.

requirements.title: Async IO ReadUntil
requirements.description: The async IO API vtable shall define a ReadUntil operation that reads until any stop character is received.
requirements.rationale: ReadUntil enables delimiter-based framing (e.g., newline-terminated protocols) without requiring the caller to implement byte-by-byte polling.

requirements.title: Async IO TryReadUntil
requirements.description: The async IO API vtable shall define a TryReadUntil operation that reads until a stop character or timeout.
requirements.rationale: TryReadUntil combines delimiter-based framing with a timeout, preventing indefinite blocking when the delimiter never arrives.

requirements.title: Async IO Write
requirements.description: The async IO API vtable shall define a Write operation that writes up to the requested number of bytes and updates the size with bytes actually written.
requirements.rationale: Write with actual-bytes-written feedback allows callers to handle partial writes, which is common with buffered or DMA-based IO on embedded targets.

requirements.title: Async IO TryWrite
requirements.description: The async IO API vtable shall define a TryWrite operation that writes with a timeout in microseconds.
requirements.rationale: TryWrite with a timeout enables bounded-time write attempts, preventing real-time tasks from stalling on a full output buffer.

requirements.title: Async IO Poll
requirements.description: The async IO API vtable shall define a Poll operation that checks for input readiness within a timeout.
requirements.rationale: Poll enables non-blocking readiness checks, allowing applications to multiplex across IO channels or perform other work while waiting for input.

requirements.title: I2C IO Root Structure
requirements.description: The I2C IO root shall embed a module root with an API vtable pointer.
requirements.rationale: An abstract I2C interface decouples sensor and peripheral drivers from platform-specific I2C hardware, enabling portability and testability across different MCU families.

requirements.title: I2C Transfer Operation
requirements.description: The I2C IO API vtable shall define a Transfer operation that processes an array of read/write messages.
requirements.rationale: A multi-message transfer operation enables compound I2C transactions (e.g., write-then-read) in a single call, which is the standard pattern for register-based peripherals.

requirements.title: I2C Message Type Discrimination
requirements.description: Each I2C message shall contain a header with a type field distinguishing read from write messages.
requirements.rationale: A type field in the message header enables the transfer operation to dispatch read vs. write behavior without requiring separate API calls for each direction.

requirements.title: I2C Device Address
requirements.description: Each I2C message header shall contain the target device address.
requirements.rationale: Including the device address in each message header allows a single transfer call to address multiple devices on the same bus, supporting multi-device transactions.

requirements.title: SPI IO Root Structure
requirements.description: The SPI IO root shall embed a module root with an API vtable pointer.
requirements.rationale: An abstract SPI interface decouples sensor and peripheral drivers from platform-specific SPI hardware, enabling portability and testability across different MCU families.

requirements.title: SPI Transaction Operation
requirements.description: The SPI IO API vtable shall define a Transaction operation for full-duplex read/write.
requirements.rationale: Full-duplex transaction is the native SPI operation model; providing it as a single call allows the platform backend to optimize DMA or hardware-level simultaneous read/write.
